document.source_title: Flak jackets and final goodbyes: Lebanon’s first responders under fire
document.summary: Recent attacks in Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of two Red Cross volunteers, Youssef Assaf and Hassan Badawi, highlighting the dangers faced by first responders in conflict zones. The UN World Health Organization reports 169 confirmed attacks on healthcare workers, leading to 116 deaths. Despite efforts to protect medical personnel, including deconfliction measures with UNIFIL, rescuers continue to be targeted, raising concerns about the safety of humanitarian workers in ongoing hostilities.
key_takeaways: Two Lebanese Red Cross volunteers were killed in recent attacks.
169 confirmed attacks on healthcare workers in Lebanon have resulted in 116 deaths.
Deconfliction measures with UNIFIL are in place but have not prevented attacks.
Deliberately targeting medical personnel is considered a war crime.
The international community is urged to protect humanitarian workers.
The withdrawal of UNIFIL raises concerns about future support for Red Cross operations.
document.why_it_matters: The ongoing violence against healthcare workers in Lebanon underscores the urgent need for international action to protect humanitarian personnel in conflict zones, as their safety is critical for providing essential medical services to affected populations.

claims.claim_text: Deliberately targeting medical personnel constitutes a war crime.
claims.evidence_text: From a human rights perspective, deliberately targeting medical personnel constitutes a war crime.

claims.claim_text: 169 confirmed attacks on healthcare workers in Lebanon.
claims.evidence_text: According to the UN World Health Organization (WHO), there have been 169 confirmed attacks on healthcare workers and facilities in Lebanon, resulting in 116 deaths.

claims.claim_text: The killing of Youssef Assaf and Hassan Badawi highlights the risks faced by first responders.
claims.evidence_text: The killing of Youssef Assaf and Hassan Badawi still haunts their volunteer colleagues who have had no explanation why they were targeted.

entities.name: Lebanese Red Cross
entities.description: A humanitarian organization providing emergency medical services in Lebanon.
entities.evidence_text: The Lebanese Red Cross works with the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), sharing coordinates and ensuring that all belligerents are informed of the paramedics’ whereabouts.

entities.name: UN World Health Organization
entities.description: A specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health.
entities.evidence_text: According to the UN World Health Organization (WHO), there have been 169 confirmed attacks on healthcare workers and facilities in Lebanon.

entities.name: International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies
entities.description: An international humanitarian organization that coordinates the activities of national Red Cross and Red Crescent societies.
entities.evidence_text: Tommaso Della Longa, spokesperson for the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC).

entities.name: Youssef Assaf
entities.description: A Lebanese Red Cross volunteer who was killed during a rescue operation.
entities.evidence_text: Youssef Assaf, who died during a rescue operation on 9 March.

entities.name: Hassan Badawi
entities.description: A Lebanese Red Cross volunteer who was killed in a drone strike.
entities.evidence_text: Hassan Badawi, killed in a drone strike on 12 April.

events.event: Death of Youssef Assaf during a rescue operation
events.evidence_text: Youssef Assaf, who died during a rescue operation on 9 March.
